rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending earlier incentive-aligned conjoint evidence from inexpensive food contexts to durable goods and addressing constraints that prior approaches did not handle, which signals substantive dependence on prior work. The cited 2005 paper is exactly that prior incentive-aligned conjoint evidence (real-purchase field experiments, BDM/lottery implementation, improved out-of-sample prediction), so this is likely foundational/comparison-relevant rather than a generic mention.
